visa_requirements: Indian passport holders with a valid US visa, green card, UK residence visa, or EU residence visa can obtain a visa-on-arrival in the UAE valid for 14 days. Others must apply for a tourist e-Visa online prior to travel through airlines or agencies.
customs_duty_free_limits: Passengers entering the UAE can carry up to 400 cigarettes, 50 cigars, and 500 grams of tobacco. Alcohol limits are up to 4 liters of alcohol or 2 cartons of beer (24 cans each). Cash limit is up to AED 60,000 (or equivalent) without declaration. Gold limit for personal use must be reasonable, typically gold jewelry up to the value of AED 20,000 (for males/females). Strictly prohibited: narcotics, poppy seeds, betel leaves, pirated content, counterfeit currency, and certain medicines without official prescriptions.
preferred_digital_wallets: Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay
tipping_culture_rules: 10% service charge usually included in restaurants; 5-10% extra is appreciated.
scam_alerts_common: Unlicensed airport taxis, overpriced souk items without bargaining.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Abu Dhabi: +971-2-4492700. Consulate General of India, Dubai: +971-4-3971222.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in public places (malls, government buildings). Modest clothing required in mosques.
photography_restrictions: Strictly illegal to photograph government buildings, military installations, or local women without consent.
major_national_closures: Ramadan (restricted daytime public eating/drinking), Eid holidays.
alcohol_availability_laws: Only available in licensed hotels, bars, and clubs. Public intoxication is strictly illegal.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a pre-arranged visa before arrival. E-visas or visa-on-arrival are only available to Indian nationals holding valid, used tourist or business visas from the US, UK, or Schengen Area, and a credit card.
customs_duty_free_limits: Alcohol, pork products, and narcotics are strictly prohibited and carry severe penalties. Passengers can bring personal items, up to 200 cigarettes, and cash/precious metals up to SAR 60,000 before declaration is required. Possession of any material contrary to Islamic teachings is banned.
preferred_digital_wallets: mada Pay, Apple Pay, STC Pay
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional; 10% is common in high-end restaurants.
scam_alerts_common: Fake guides around holy sites, unlicensed taxis.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Riyadh: +966-11-4884144. Consulate General of India, Jeddah: +966-12-2614093.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly. Abaya is not legally required for foreign women but shoulders and knees must be covered.
photography_restrictions: Illegal to photograph government and military complexes. Do not take photos of people without asking.
alcohol_availability_laws: Strictly illegal. Zero tolerance for possession, consumption, or import of alcohol.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders require a pre-approved visa (e.g., B1/B2 tourist/business visa) from a US Embassy or Consulate before traveling. The USA does not offer visa-on-arrival or e-Visas for Indian citizens.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes, 100 cigars, and 1 liter of alcoholic beverages (for travelers aged 21 or older). Cash limit is USD 10,000 or equivalent; amounts exceeding this must be declared on Form FinCEN 105. Strictly prohibited: meat, fresh fruits, vegetables, seeds, plants, and unapproved medicines.
tipping_culture_rules: Expected 15-20% at restaurants, bars, and for services.
scam_alerts_common: Fake ticket sellers near major attractions, taxi overcharging, pickpocketing in crowded areas.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Washington DC: +1-202-939-7000. Consulate General of India, New York: +1-212-774-0600.
dress_code_restrictions: No specific laws, but shirts and shoes are generally required in establishments.
photography_restrictions: Legal in public places. Commercial use or drones require licenses. No photographing border control zones.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
monsoon_cyclone_months: Atlantic Hurricane Season: June to November
major_national_closures: Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, New Year's Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for age 21+. Open containers are banned on public streets in most states.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens can travel to Thailand visa-free under current promotional schemes (valid up to 60 days) or apply for a Visa-on-arrival (VoA) for 15 days, or apply for an e-Visa online prior to travel.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 200 cigarettes or 250g of tobacco, and 1 liter of alcohol. Cash limit: must possess at least 10,000 THB per person or 20,000 THB per family in cash to clear immigration. Strictly prohibited: e-cigarettes, vapes, and shisha. Carrying electronic cigarettes or vapes into Thailand is illegal and can lead to heavy fines or imprisonment.
power_plug_types: Type A, Type B, Type C, Type O
tipping_culture_rules: Not traditional, but small tips (20-50 THB) are appreciated in hotels and for drivers.
scam_alerts_common: Tuk-tuk scams claiming temples are closed, jet ski rental damage scams, overpriced taxi meters.
dress_code_restrictions: Strict dress codes for temples: shoulders and knees must be fully covered. No crop tops or beachwear.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military zones. Drones must be registered with CAAT. Don't take photos of temples during prayers.
major_national_closures: Songkran (Thai New Year in April), King's Birthday.
alcohol_availability_laws: Sold only between 11 AM - 2 PM and 5 PM - Midnight. Banned on religious holidays.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a tourist visa before entering Singapore. Visas can be applied online through authorized agents. Travelers must submit the SG Arrival Card (SGAC) online within 3 days prior to arrival.
customs_duty_free_limits: No duty-free allowance for tobacco or cigarettes—all imported tobacco products must be declared and tax paid. Alcohol allowance is up to 2 liters (combination of spirits, wine, and beer). Cash limit is SGD 20,000 (or equivalent); amounts higher must be declared. Strictly prohibited: chewing gum, e-cigarettes, vapes, firecrackers, and replica weapons.
tipping_culture_rules: No tipping/offensive. 10% service charge is already included in restaurant bills.
scam_alerts_common: High price listings in tourist areas (Sim Lim Square tech scams), fake rental accommodation listings.
dress_code_restrictions: No dress codes except covering shoulders and knees when visiting mosques and temples.
photography_restrictions: Drones must be licensed. Photography in mass transit stations is restricted without authorization.
peak_tourism_months: December to January, June to July
major_national_closures: Chinese New Year (many retail closures), National Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Public consumption of alcohol is banned between 10:30 PM and 7:00 AM.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders require a Standard Visitor Visa to visit the UK. The visa must be applied for and processed in advance via VFS Global. It permits stays of up to 6 months.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowances include 200 cigarettes or 50 cigars, and up to 4 liters of spirits or 9 liters of fortified wine. Cash limit: travelers must declare cash of GBP 10,000 or more (or equivalent) when entering or leaving the UK from outside the UK. Prohibited: meat, milk, and other animal products from outside the EU.
preferred_digital_wallets: Apple Pay, Google Pay, Contactless Cards
tipping_culture_rules: Expected 10-12.5% in sit-down restaurants if a service charge is not already added.
scam_alerts_common: Unlicensed minicabs, pickpocketing around London Underground, ticket scalpers.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Strict laws against filming people without permission for commercial use. Drones prohibited in royal parks.
shoulder_season_months: April to June, September to October
major_national_closures: Christmas Day (entire public transit shuts down), Good Friday.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Supermarket sales restricted overnight in Scotland.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens can travel to Qatar visa-free under the Visa Waiver program for up to 30 days. Requirements include a passport valid for 6 months, a confirmed return ticket, and a confirmed hotel booking through the Discover Qatar website.
customs_duty_free_limits: Import of alcohol, pork products, and religious materials contrary to Islamic beliefs is strictly prohibited. Duty-free allowance includes up to 400 cigarettes. Cash/valuables limit is QAR 50,000; amounts exceeding this must be declared.
tipping_culture_rules: 10-15% is common if service charge is not included.
scam_alerts_common: Overcharging by airport taxis without running meters.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in public. Swimwear is only allowed on hotel beaches and pools.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military, police, or local families without permission.
alcohol_availability_laws: Strictly limited to licensed hotel bars. Public drinking is highly illegal.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Temporary Resident Visa (visitor visa) to travel to Canada. It must be applied for and processed in advance online. The visa typically allows stays of up to 6 months per entry.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes, 50 cigars, 1.5 liters of wine, or 1.14 liters of spirits. Cash limit: travelers carrying CAD 10,000 or more (or equivalent) must declare it to the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) on arrival. Fresh fruits, vegetables, and meats must be declared.
tipping_culture_rules: Standard tipping is 15-20% on pre-tax bills.
scam_alerts_common: Grandparent scam calls targeting travelers, fake rental listings, cab cash-swap scams.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: High Commission of India, Ottawa: +613-744-3751. Consulate General, Toronto: +1-416-960-0751.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Drones strictly regulated. Do not photograph children without parental consent.
peak_tourism_months: June to August (Summer), December to February (Skiing)
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Ages 18 or 19 (varies by province). Open alcohol in public is generally banned.

visa_requirements: Indian nationals must obtain a tourist visa prior to travel, sponsored by a Kuwaiti resident, hotel, or travel agency. Visa-on-arrival is not general for Indian citizens unless they have GCC residency under specific terms.
customs_duty_free_limits: Kuwait enforces a absolute prohibition on alcohol, pork products, and narcotics. Duty-free allowance includes up to 500 cigarettes. Cash limit is KWD 3,000 (or equivalent) in cash or valuables, above which declaration is mandatory.
tipping_culture_rules: 10% service charge is usually added; extra 5-10% is customary.
scam_alerts_common: Unmetered airport taxis, overpricing by street merchants.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly. Cover shoulders and knees. Swimwear strictly confined to private resort pools.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph government palaces, security checkpoints, or local citizens.
alcohol_availability_laws: Completely dry country. Alcohol is entirely banned; penalties include jail and deportation.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders can visit Oman visa-free for up to 14 days, provided they have a hotel booking, travel insurance, and a return ticket. Otherwise, they can apply for an e-Visa online prior to travel.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 400 cigarettes and up to 2 bottles (or 2 liters) of alcohol (for non-Muslims only). Cash limit: OMR 6,000 or equivalent must be declared. Prohibited: offensive literature, illegal drugs, and weapons.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is not required but 10% is standard in tourist areas.
scam_alerts_common: Overcharging for airport transfer or private 4x4 desert tours without agreements.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in public. Abayas are not mandatory for visitors, but tight clothing should be avoided.
photography_restrictions: Strict ban on photographing military compounds, government offices, or Omani women.
monsoon_cyclone_months: Khareef Season in Salalah: July to September
alcohol_availability_laws: Non-Muslims can consume alcohol in licensed hotels/bars. Public drinking is illegal.

visa_requirements: Indian nationals are eligible for visa-free entry to Malaysia for stays of up to 30 days under current policies. Travelers must fill out the Malaysia Digital Arrival Card (MDAC) online within 3 days prior to arrival.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 1 liter of alcohol and up to 200 cigarettes (or 225g of tobacco). Cash limit: amounts exceeding USD 10,000 or equivalent must be declared to customs. Severe penalties, including death penalty, for drug trafficking.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is not customary; 10% service charge is usually included.
scam_alerts_common: Card-skimming, bag snatching by motorists, taxi drivers refusing to run meters.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: High Commission of India, Kuala Lumpur: +60-3-20933510.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees when visiting mosques and temples. Remove shoes inside temples.
photography_restrictions: No photographing military sites. Drones must be licensed by CAAM.
peak_tourism_months: December to January (Festivals), June to August
shoulder_season_months: March to May, September to November
monsoon_cyclone_months: East Coast monsoon: November to March; West Coast: September to November
major_national_closures: Chinese New Year, Hari Raya (Eid), National Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Highly taxed. Widely available except in Kelantan and Terengganu.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders must apply for a Visitor Visa (Subclass 600) online prior to travel. This visa allows tourism and business visits for up to 3, 6, or 12 months.
customs_duty_free_limits: Australia has extremely strict biosecurity laws. You must declare all food, plant material, and animal products. Failing to declare can result in immediate fines up to AUD 6,260 or visa cancellation. Duty-free allowance includes 25 cigarettes (or 25g of tobacco) and 2.25 liters of alcohol. Cash limit is AUD 10,000.
preferred_digital_wallets: Apple Pay, Google Pay, Contactless Cards
tipping_culture_rules: No tipping/offensive. Optional 10% for fine dining for excellent service.
scam_alerts_common: Fake tour operators online, pickpocketing at major beaches, overcharging taxi apps.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: High Commission of India, Canberra: +61-2-62733999. Consulate, Sydney: +61-2-92232702.
dress_code_restrictions: None. Very casual public dress standard.
photography_restrictions: Drones banned near beaches with crowds. Standard public privacy laws apply.
shoulder_season_months: March to May, September to November
monsoon_cyclone_months: Northern tropical cyclone season: November to April
major_national_closures: Good Friday, Christmas Day, ANZAC Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Open bottles are banned in public parks/streets in most cities.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a valid visa to travel to Bangladesh. Visas can be applied for at the Bangladesh High Commission/Visa Application Center in India. Visa-on-arrival is not generally available for tourist purposes.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 200 cigarettes and up to 2 bottles of alcoholic beverages (non-Muslims only). Foreign currency exceeding USD 5,000 must be declared at customs on arrival.
power_plug_types: Type C, Type D, Type G, Type K
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is common in tourist hotels and restaurants (around 5-10% of bill).
scam_alerts_common: Rickshaw fare gouging, fake guides at monuments, pickpocketing in dense crowds.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly. Cover shoulders and knees. Loose, non-revealing clothing recommended.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph airports, military bases, or police stations.
major_national_closures: Eid al-Fitr, Eid al-Adha, Durga Puja.
alcohol_availability_laws: Highly restricted. Only sold in designated bars and 5-star hotel bars.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders can apply for an e-VoA (electronic Visa on Arrival) online before travel or purchase a Visa-on-Arrival (VoA) at the airport counter for IDR 500,000 (~35 USD), valid for 30 days. Travelers must also pay the Bali Tourist Levy online before arrival.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes or 50 cigars, and up to 1 liter of alcohol. Cash limit: cash amounts of IDR 100,000,000 or more (approx. 6,500 USD) must be declared. Extremely strict drug laws: drug possession or trafficking carries the death penalty.
tipping_culture_rules: Not mandatory, but rounding up the bill or leaving 5-10% is customary in tourist venues.
scam_alerts_common: Money changer scams (short-changing), inflated pricing for scooter damages, local temple entry guides overcharging.
dress_code_restrictions: Temples require sarong and sash. Swimwear is illegal outside beaches and beach clubs.
photography_restrictions: Drones must not fly near temples or crowds. Respect local religious ceremonies.
peak_tourism_months: July to August, December to January
shoulder_season_months: April to June, September to November
major_national_closures: Nyepi (Bali Day of Silence - everything shuts down, including the airport).
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 21+. Alcohol is sold in restaurants and convenience stores.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens can visit Sri Lanka visa-free or by obtaining an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) online or Visa-on-arrival. Please verify the current status of visa fee waivers as Sri Lanka frequently adjusts them for Indian tourists.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 1.5 liters of spirits and 2 bottles of wine. Note: there is no duty-free allowance for cigarettes or tobacco products; importing them is illegal without a license and subject to confiscation. Cash limits: currency exceeding USD 15,000 must be declared.
power_plug_types: Type D, Type G, Type M
tipping_culture_rules: 10% service charge is usually included; extra 5-10% is expected for good service.
tourist_transit_pass_name: None (Train and Tuk-Tuk cards are cash-based)
scam_alerts_common: Tuk-tuk drivers overcharging tourists, fake spice garden tours, overpriced gem shops.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in temples. Remove shoes and hats before entering. Buddhist tattoos must be covered.
photography_restrictions: Strictly illegal to pose for photos with your back facing a Buddha statue.
peak_tourism_months: December to April (West Coast), May to September (East Coast)
monsoon_cyclone_months: South-West monsoon: May to September; North-East: October to January
major_national_closures: Sinhala & Tamil New Year (April), monthly Poya days (full moon day closures).
alcohol_availability_laws: Widely available, except on Poya days when selling alcohol is illegal nationwide.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Germany. Applications must be submitted through VFS Global. The visa permits travel across the entire Schengen zone for up to 90 days within a 180-day period.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowances from non-EU countries include 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits (over 22% ABV), and personal goods up to the value of EUR 430. Cash limit: cash of EUR 10,000 or more (or equivalent) must be declared in writing at customs.
tipping_culture_rules: Rounding up the bill ( Trinkgeld ), usually 5-10% of total amount.
scam_alerts_common: Petition signing scams (pickpocketing), fake transit ticket checkers, Shell game scams on public streets.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Berlin: +49-30-257950. Consulate General, Munich: +49-89-2102390.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Strict privacy laws (DSGVO). Illegal to photograph individuals without their explicit consent.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October (Oktoberfest is peak in Munich)
major_national_closures: Sundays (most supermarkets and shops are closed legally), Christmas Day, Unity Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for age 16+ (beer/wine), 18+ (spirits). Public drinking is generally legal.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens can apply for an e-Visa online prior to travel through the official Bahrain eVisa portal. Tourist e-Visas are typically issued for stays of 14 days or 30 days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 400 cigarettes, 50 cigars, and up to 1 liter of spirits or 6 cans of beer (for non-Muslims). Cash limit is BHD 10,000 (or equivalent); amounts exceeding this must be declared.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is not mandatory, but 10% is commonly added in upscale restaurants.
scam_alerts_common: Airport taxi drivers not turning on taximeters.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in public buildings, mosques, and traditional markets.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military, government assets, or local residents without consent.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for non-Muslims aged 18+. Available in licensed hotels and stores.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders require a tourist visa to visit Japan. They can apply for a paper tourist visa through VFS Global or check eligibility for the eVisa system. The visa must be obtained prior to departure.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 200 cigarettes (or 50 cigars), 3 bottles of alcohol (approx. 760ml per bottle), and up to 2 ounces of perfume. Cash/gold limit: carrying cash or gold exceeding 1 million JPY in value must be declared. Strictly prohibited: illegal drugs, firearms, counterfeit goods, and meat products (such as beef, pork, or poultry items).
tipping_culture_rules: No tipping/offensive. Exceptional service is covered by restaurant seating charges.
tourist_transit_pass_name: Suica Card, Pasmo Card, JR Pass
scam_alerts_common: Taut/Spruiker scams in nightlife areas (Kabukicho drinks overcharging), fake taxi apps.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Tokyo: +81-3-3262-2391. Consulate General, Osaka: +81-6-6261-7299.
dress_code_restrictions: None. Very modest general styling is standard.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military zones. Avoid taking photos of geishas in Kyoto or locals without permission.
peak_tourism_months: Cherry Blossom Season (March to April), Autumn Leaves (October to November)
major_national_closures: New Year's Oshogatsu (Jan 1-3, major closures), Golden Week (late April to early May).
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 20+. Available 24/7 in convenience stores and vending machines.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders require a Schengen Visa to visit France. Applications must be submitted in advance through VFS Global. The visa permits travel across the Schengen zone for up to 90 days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits, and goods up to EUR 430 in value. Cash limit: cash or assets of EUR 10,000 or more must be declared to customs. Possession of counterfeit luxury goods is a serious offense in France and can result in severe fines.
preferred_digital_wallets: Apple Pay, Google Pay, Contactless Cards
tipping_culture_rules: Service charge is included in the bill. Small tips (round up or 5%) are appreciated for good service.
scam_alerts_common: Gold ring trick, petition signature scams (pickpockets), cup game scams around Eiffel Tower.
dress_code_restrictions: Face-covering garments (such as burqas/niqabs) are legally banned in public spaces.
photography_restrictions: Illegal to use photos of people for commercial use without consent. Banned drone usage in Paris.
peak_tourism_months: July to August, May to June
shoulder_season_months: September to October, March to April
major_national_closures: Labour Day (May 1, total closures), Christmas Day, Bastille Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Public consumption is restricted in certain municipal zones overnight.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders can visit Russia by obtaining a tourist visa in advance, or apply for a Unified e-Visa online, which allows a stay of up to 16 days. Visas must be obtained before travel.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes up to 200 cigarettes or 50 cigars, and up to 3 liters of alcohol (travelers aged 18 or older). Cash limit: cash of USD 10,000 or more (or equivalent) must be declared. Prohibited: weapons, narcotics, and certain medicines without prescriptions.
tipping_culture_rules: 10% is standard in restaurants and bars (tip in cash directly to waiter).
scam_alerts_common: Fake police officers checking documents (demanding bribes), taxi overcharging without apps.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Moscow: +7-495-7834100. Consulate, St. Petersburg: +7-812-6407222.
dress_code_restrictions: Head coverings (scarves) for women and hats off for men inside Orthodox churches.
photography_restrictions: Strictly illegal to photograph airports, train stations, military sites, and government offices.
peak_tourism_months: June to August (White Nights in St. Petersburg)
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Store sales of alcohol are banned between 11 PM and 8 AM.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens can easily apply for an e-Visa online which is processed in 3 working days and allows single or multiple entries up to 90 days. Direct flights exist from major Indian cities.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes, 1.5 liters of spirits (over 22% ABV), and personal jewelry up to 300 grams. Cash over USD 5,000 must be declared.
power_plug_types: Type A, Type C, Type G
tipping_culture_rules: Not expected but highly appreciated in tourist hubs; round up or leave 10% for service.
scam_alerts_common: Shoe cleaning scams in Hanoi, overcharging by street vendors, unauthorized taxi meters.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Hanoi: +84-24-38244989. Consulate, Ho Chi Minh City: +84-28-37442400.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly. Cover knees and shoulders at temples, pagodas, and mausoleums.
photography_restrictions: Strictly no military sites, government offices, or border posts.
shoulder_season_months: May to June, September to October
monsoon_cyclone_months: June to November (typhoons in central regions)
major_national_closures: Tet (Lunar New Year - country shuts down for a week in Jan/Feb).
alcohol_availability_laws: Very relaxed; alcohol available everywhere, open containers generally tolerated.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa prior to arrival. However, travelers with a valid used visa from the US, Japan, Australia, Canada, Schengen, UK, or Singapore can enter visa-free for up to 14 days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 400 cigarettes (or 50 cigars) and 2 bottles of alcohol (max 1 liter each). Currency over USD 10,000 must be declared.
power_plug_types: Type A, Type B, Type C
tipping_culture_rules: Usually 10% of the bill is appreciated if not included in service charge.
scam_alerts_common: Overpriced tricycle rides, airport taxi luggage scams, fake tour guides in island ports.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in churches. Swimwear is for beaches only.
photography_restrictions: No photographing police or military checkpoints. Drones require CAAP registration.
major_national_closures: Holy Week (Easter), Christmas Day, All Saints Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Dry zones are established during elections and some local festivals.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens are granted a free 30-day Visa-on-arrival. Requirements include a passport valid for 1 month, a confirmed return ticket, and a confirmed resort/hotel booking.
customs_duty_free_limits: Import of alcohol, pork products, and religious idols is strictly prohibited. Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes. Currency limit: declare amounts over USD 20,000.
tipping_culture_rules: 10% service charge is added to bills. Extra cash tips are appreciated by resort staff.
scam_alerts_common: Overpriced speedboats in local islands, fake pearl sales.
dress_code_restrictions: Modest clothing required on local inhabited islands. Bikinis and swimwear are restricted to private resort islands.
photography_restrictions: Drones must be cleared with resort management and MNDF.
major_national_closures: Ramadan (local restaurants closed during the day).
alcohol_availability_laws: Strictly illegal on local islands. Allowed only in tourist resorts and safari boats.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens do not require a visa to enter, reside, or work in Nepal under the 1950 Treaty. Valid Indian Passport or Voter ID is required for flight arrivals.
customs_duty_free_limits: Personal items, up to 200 cigarettes, 1 bottle of alcohol (max 1.15L). Carrying Indian currency notes of denominations Rs 200, Rs 500 and Rs 2000 is illegal; only Rs 100 or below are allowed.
power_plug_types: Type C, Type D, Type M
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is common in tourist areas (usually 10% service charge is added).
scam_alerts_common: Fake rescue scams for trekkers, overpriced taxi fares at Kathmandu airport.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in temples. Remove shoes before entering sacred sites.
photography_restrictions: Photography in some inner temple complexes is strictly forbidden. Drones require CAAN approval.
peak_tourism_months: October to November, March to April
alcohol_availability_laws: Legally restricted to licensed shops and bars; alcohol sale banned on election days.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require an entry permit. Visas must be pre-arranged, and visitors must pay a Sustainable Development Fee (SDF) of INR 1,200 per night per person.
customs_duty_free_limits: Bringing tobacco products into Bhutan is legal for tourists but heavily taxed (up to 200%). Sale of tobacco inside Bhutan is banned. Max 1 liter of alcohol allowed.
power_plug_types: Type C, Type D, Type G
tipping_culture_rules: Not expected but highly appreciated by guides and drivers.
scam_alerts_common: Inflated rates for private vehicle rentals in Thimphu.
dress_code_restrictions: Strict modest dress. Long pants/skirts required. Collared shirts needed to enter Dzongs (fortresses).
photography_restrictions: Strictly illegal to take photos inside the inner sanctuaries of Dzongs and temples.
peak_tourism_months: October to December, March to May
alcohol_availability_laws: Widely available, except on Tuesdays which are designated dry days nationwide.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. E-visas can be obtained online prior to travel, and Visa-on-arrival is available at Siem Reap and Phnom Penh international airports.
customs_duty_free_limits: Allowances include 400 cigarettes, 1.5 liters of spirits, and personal goods up to USD 500. Cash over USD 10,000 must be declared.
power_plug_types: Type A, Type C, Type G
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is not mandatory but highly appreciated by service staff and drivers.
scam_alerts_common: Short-changing at local markets, corrupt border guards asking for visual bribes, fake orphanage scams.
dress_code_restrictions: Strict dress codes for Angkor Wat: knees and shoulders must be fully covered.
photography_restrictions: No photographing military setups or drone flights near temple complexes without permits.
alcohol_availability_laws: Very relaxed; alcohol is widely sold in marts and restaurants at low prices.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens do not require a visa for stays up to 14 days but must complete a Pre-arrival Registration (PAR) online prior to departure.
customs_duty_free_limits: Strict limits: up to 19 cigarettes, 1 bottle of wine/liquor (max 1L). Severe penalties for bringing CBD products (cannabidiol) which is illegal.
preferred_digital_wallets: Octopus Pay, Apple Pay, WeChat Pay, Alipay
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional; taxi fares are usually rounded up to the nearest dollar.
scam_alerts_common: Overpriced herbal medicines, fake brand luxury goods, seafood price manipulation.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Consulate General of India, Hong Kong: +852-39709922.
dress_code_restrictions: No specific codes. Casual wear is normal.
photography_restrictions: Generally legal. No photography in airports security lines or court houses.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Alcohol sold in 7-Eleven and bars 24/7.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. Visas must be applied for and processed in advance via VFS Global. E-visas are only available for group tourists under specific schemes.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes, 1 bottle of alcohol (max 1L, value under USD 400), and 60ml perfume. Cash over USD 10,000 must be declared.
preferred_digital_wallets: Kakao Pay, Naver Pay, Samsung Pay
tipping_culture_rules: No tipping/offensive. Excellent service is standard without fees.
scam_alerts_common: Taut scams in bars in Itaewon, taxi drivers overcharging late-night passengers.
dress_code_restrictions: None. Very fashion-forward yet modest standards are standard in public.
photography_restrictions: Strict camera laws: mobile shutter sounds are mandatory by law to prevent non-consensual photography.
peak_tourism_months: September to November (Autumn), April to June (Spring)
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 19+. Widely sold in convenience stores 24/7 (Soju is very cheap).

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. However, they can apply for a free Travel Authorization Certificate (TAC) online if they hold a valid visa/residency from US, Canada, Japan, Schengen, UK, Australia, or NZ.
customs_duty_free_limits: Duty-free allowance includes 200 cigarettes or 25 cigars, and 1 liter of alcoholic beverages. Strict ban on importing meat/pork products from abroad.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is not expected; restaurants usually add a 10% service fee.
scam_alerts_common: Overpricing at night markets, unregistered tour guide fees.
dress_code_restrictions: None. Very casual public dress codes.
photography_restrictions: No photographing military facilities. Drones must comply with CAA rules.
peak_tourism_months: October to December, March to May
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Sold at all marts 24/7.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders can apply for an ASAN e-Visa online. It is processed within 3 hours (urgent) or 3 days (standard), valid for a stay up to 30 days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Allows up to 200 cigarettes, 3 liters of alcohol. Cash over USD 10,000 must be declared at customs on entry.
tipping_culture_rules: 10% service charge is usually included; leaving 5-10% extra is appreciated.
scam_alerts_common: Taxi price manipulation (always use apps), inflated menu pricing in bars around Fountain Square.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover shoulders and knees in religious sites. Casual dress is standard in Baku.
photography_restrictions: Strictly forbidden to photograph metro stations, military bases, or oil refineries.
peak_tourism_months: April to June, September to October
major_national_closures: Novruz (Spring New Year in March), Republic Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Widely available and sold in supermarkets and bars at all hours.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders can visit Kazakhstan visa-free for tourist or business trips up to 14 days per entry. Total cumulative stay cannot exceed 42 days in any 180-day period.
customs_duty_free_limits: Limits include up to 200 cigarettes, 3 liters of alcohol. Cash over USD 10,000 must be declared at customs on arrival.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional; 10% is usually added as a service fee on restaurant bills.
scam_alerts_common: Private taxi scams at Almaty Airport (always use Yandex Go app), inflated fees for pictures with animals in parks.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Astana: +7-7172-925700. Representative Office, Almaty: +7-727-2784400.
dress_code_restrictions: Remove shoes and cover knees/shoulders inside mosques and churches.
photography_restrictions: No photographing airports, transit hubs, military installations, or border guards.
peak_tourism_months: December to February (Skiing), June to August (Trekking)
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 21+. Alcohol sales banned in stores after 11 PM.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. E-visas can be applied online through the official portal, offering a stay of up to 30 days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Allows up to 200 cigarettes, 2 liters of alcohol. Declare all cash exceeding USD 2,000 at customs. Keep custom declaration slips until departure.
tipping_culture_rules: 10% service charge usually included; tips are not strictly expected.
scam_alerts_common: Unofficial currency exchangers (always use banks), taxi driver overcharging.
dress_code_restrictions: Cover knees and shoulders when visiting historical madrasahs and active mosques.
photography_restrictions: Generally legal. Avoid military zones and state buildings.
peak_tourism_months: April to May, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 20+. Sold in specialty stores and restaurants.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. Visas must be applied online or via VFS. However, valid visa/residency holders of US, Canada, UK, Schengen, Australia, Japan, or GCC countries can enter visa-free for 90 days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 3 liters of wine or 4 liters of beer. Strict ban on carrying medicines containing codeine or pseudoephedrine without prescription translation.
tipping_culture_rules: Usually 10-15% is automatically added to bills in cafes and restaurants.
scam_alerts_common: Overpriced bills at strip clubs or local bars, fake tour operators offering Caucasus day-trips.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Yerevan (concurrently accredited to Georgia): +374-10-539173. Honorary Consul, Tbilisi: +995-322-251703.
dress_code_restrictions: Orthodox churches require women to cover heads/knees and men to wear long pants and remove hats.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military zones. Drones require GCAA permits.
peak_tourism_months: July to August (Summer), January to February (Ski)
shoulder_season_months: May to June, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Very liberal. Wine is a national heritage; public consumption generally accepted.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. Visas must be applied online as an e-Visa or on arrival if holding residency/visa from GCC countries, US, Canada, Schengen, or UK.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 3 liters of alcohol. Declaring cash exceeding USD 10,000 is mandatory on arrival.
tipping_culture_rules: 10% is standard in Yerevan restaurants if service charge is not included.
scam_alerts_common: Inflated taxi fares, souvenir pricing overcharge.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly when entering monasteries. Women are advised to cover their heads.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military checkpoints or border crossings near Azerbaijan/Turkey.
peak_tourism_months: June to August, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Widely available and sold in supermarkets and bars.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Switzerland. Applications must be submitted through VFS Global. The visa permits travel across Switzerland and the Schengen zone.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 250 cigarettes, 5 liters of low-alcohol beverages (under 18% ABV) or 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR/CHF 10,000 or more is required.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is not required as service charge is legally included in the bill, but rounding up to the nearest CHF 5-10 is common.
tourist_transit_pass_name: Swiss Travel Pass, Half Fare Card
scam_alerts_common: None (extremely low crime rate, watch for pickpockets in Swiss trains).
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military setups or use commercial drones without FOCA clearance.
peak_tourism_months: June to August (Summer), December to March (Skiing)
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Ages 16+ (beer/wine), 18+ (spirits). Public drinking legal.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Italy. Applications must be processed via VFS Global.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash over EUR 10,000 is required upon entry.
power_plug_types: Type C, Type F, Type L
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional; restaurant bills often include a cover charge ('coperto') of 1-3 EUR.
scam_alerts_common: Pickpocketing around Rome Colosseum/Termini, rose-giving scams, fake taxi meters.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Rome: +39-06-4884642. Consulate General, Milan: +39-02-8057691.
dress_code_restrictions: Strict dress codes for churches (Vatican/St. Peter's): shoulders and knees must be fully covered.
photography_restrictions: Drones banned in historical city centers. Commercial photography requires permits.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Public consumption generally tolerated except in tourist areas overnight.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit the Netherlands. Applications must be submitted through VFS Global.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR 10,000 or more is mandatory.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional; rounding up or leaving 5-10% is common for good service.
scam_alerts_common: Bicycle theft, pickpockets around Red Light District, fake ticket sellers at Central Station.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph workers in the Red Light District windows (highly restricted/fines).
peak_tourism_months: April to May (Tulip season), June to August (Summer)
shoulder_season_months: September to November, February to March
major_national_closures: King's Day (April 27 - citywide closures/festivals), Christmas Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Open containers are banned in certain public areas designated by municipal boards.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Spain. Applications must be submitted through BLS International.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR 10,000 or more is required upon entry.
tipping_culture_rules: Optional; leaving 5-10% in restaurants is standard if service is outstanding.
scam_alerts_common: Pickpocketing on Barcelona Metro and Las Ramblas, fake petition signers, shell game tricks.
dress_code_restrictions: Beachwear is prohibited on city streets in Barcelona and Palma (fines apply).
photography_restrictions: Standard EU rules. Drones require AESA registration and local authorization.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
major_national_closures: National Day (October 12), Christmas Day, Good Friday.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Public drinking ('botellón') is illegal and subject to police fines.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Austria. Applications must be submitted through VFS Global.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR 10,000 or more is required upon entry.
tipping_culture_rules: Expected to tip 5-10% by rounding up the bill at cafes and restaurants.
scam_alerts_common: None (very low crime rates, watch for pickpockets at Christmas Markets).
dress_code_restrictions: Modest clothing recommended for opera houses and cathedral visits.
photography_restrictions: Drones must be registered with Austro Control. No commercial filming in palaces without permits.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
major_national_closures: Sundays (virtually all shops and supermarkets closed), Christmas Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for age 16+ (beer/wine), 18+ (spirits). Public drinking legal.

visa_requirements: Indian nationals must apply for a visa. E-visas are only available if holding a valid, used visa/residency from US, UK, Ireland, or Schengen countries.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 600 cigarettes, 1 liter of strong spirits (over 22% ABV) or 2 liters of weak alcohol. Declare cash over EUR 10,000.
preferred_digital_wallets: Apple Pay (not supported locally, use physical cards), Troy, Papara
tipping_culture_rules: Expected 5-10% in cash directly to the waiter in restaurants; not included in bills.
scam_alerts_common: Overpriced shoe shine brush-drop trick, fake carpet dealers inviting tourists to tea, inflated bar bills in Istanbul.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Ankara: +90-312-4382110. Consulate General, Istanbul: +90-212-2962105.
dress_code_restrictions: Strict modest dress for mosques: women must cover heads/shoulders/knees; men must wear long pants.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military, police, or state checkpoints. Drones require SHGM permission.
peak_tourism_months: June to August, April to May (Spring)
alcohol_availability_laws: Widely available; sale restricted in retail stores after 10 PM.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Greece. Applications must be submitted through GVCW (Greece Visa Center World).
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR 10,000 or more is required upon entry.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional but leaving 5-10% cash is common in traditional tavernas.
scam_alerts_common: Overpricing at beachside bars in Mykonos/Santorini, unlicensed taxi meters, pickpocketing in Athens metro.
dress_code_restrictions: Monastery visits (e.g. Meteora) require shoulders and knees to be covered; skirts for women, trousers for men.
photography_restrictions: Standard EU rules. Strictly illegal to photograph military bases.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
major_national_closures: Assumption of Mary (August 15), Easter Sunday, Christmas Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Widely available in supermarkets, tavernas, and kiosk stands.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit the Czech Republic. Applications must be submitted through VFS Global.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR 10,000 or more (or equivalent) is mandatory.
tipping_culture_rules: Standard tipping is 10% in tourist restaurants if a service fee is not added.
scam_alerts_common: Currency exchange scams (always use official banks or cards), overpriced taxi apps, pickpockets on Charles Bridge.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Standard EU rules. No drones in urban centers.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Beer is highly popular, widely sold, and often cheaper than water.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Norway. Applications must be submitted through VFS Global.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits and 1.5 liters of wine. Declare cash over NOK 25,000.
preferred_digital_wallets: Vipps (requires local ID, tourists use Apple/Google Pay), Apple Pay, Google Pay
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional; rounding up to the nearest NOK 50-100 is appreciated in restaurants.
scam_alerts_common: None (very low crime rate, watch for slippery paths in northern winter).
dress_code_restrictions: Dress appropriately for outdoor weather. No public dress code regulations.
photography_restrictions: Respect private property. National parks have specific drone zones.
peak_tourism_months: June to August (Midnight Sun), December to February (Northern Lights)
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
major_national_closures: Constitution Day (May 17 - major closures), Christmas Day, Easter.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+ (beer/wine), 20+ (spirits). Hard spirits sold only in state-run Vinmonopolet stores.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens are granted a free Visa-on-arrival for a stay of up to 60 days. Requirements include a passport valid for 6 months, return ticket, and sufficient funds.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes or 250g of tobacco, and 1 liter of spirits. Declare currency over USD 10,000.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is optional; 10% is appreciated in restaurants and for private tour guides.
scam_alerts_common: Overpriced private beach activities, unlicensed street guides.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: High Commission of India, Port Louis: +230-2112258.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly when visiting temples/shrines. Swimwear is for resort pool and beach zones only.
photography_restrictions: Respect local privacy. Drones require Department of Civil Aviation permits.
major_national_closures: Abolition of Slavery Day (Feb 1), Cavadee, Diwali.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Sold widely in supermarkets and hotels.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require an Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) prior to travel, applied online. It replaces the traditional visa system.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 bottle of alcoholic beverage. Prohibited: Single-use plastic bags are strictly illegal in Kenya (carrying them carries severe fines).
preferred_digital_wallets: M-Pesa (widely accepted for all payments), Airtel Money
tipping_culture_rules: Expected tip is 10% in restaurants and USD 10-15 per day for safari drivers/guides.
scam_alerts_common: Street pickpockets in Nairobi, fake wildlife safari tour companies online.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly outside resorts. Camouflage clothing is illegal for civilians in Kenya.
photography_restrictions: Do not photograph military, government, or infrastructure zones. Ask before photographing tribal communities.
peak_tourism_months: July to October (Great Wildebeest Migration), January to February
alcohol_availability_laws: Sold in licensed bars, hotels, and supermarkets; restricted overnight.

visa_requirements: Indian nationals must apply for a tourist visa prior to travel. Visas can be submitted through VFS Global. Standard processing takes up to 10-15 working days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Allows up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits and 2 liters of wine. Declare cash over USD 10,000 on entry.
power_plug_types: Type D, Type M, Type N
tipping_culture_rules: Expected 10-15% on sit-down restaurant bills and small cash tips for parking attendants.
scam_alerts_common: Card skimming at ATMs, driveway robberies, fake parking attendants in city centers.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: High Commission of India, Pretoria: +27-12-3425392. Consulate, Johannesburg: +27-11-4828484.
dress_code_restrictions: None. Very casual dress norms.
photography_restrictions: Respect private property. Do not film government or military facilities.
peak_tourism_months: December to January (Summer), June to August (Winter safaris)
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to November
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Store sales restricted on Sundays and public holidays.

visa_requirements: Indian passport holders do not require a visa. A free Visitor's Permit is issued on arrival for stays up to 30 days. Requirements: return ticket, confirmed stay, and travel authorization.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits and 2 liters of wine. Strict protection of local flora/fauna; do not carry coco de mer nuts out without official permits.
tipping_culture_rules: Optional; most service charges (5-10%) are already added to hotel and restaurant bills.
scam_alerts_common: Overpricing by airport taxis (always agree on a price first), inflated prices for island boat tours.
dress_code_restrictions: Swimwear is limited to beaches and pool decks. Dress smartly for dining.
photography_restrictions: No photographing military sites or airports. Ask resort management before drone usage.
peak_tourism_months: April to May, October to November
shoulder_season_months: December to March, June to September
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Alcohol sold in licensed stores and hotels.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. They can apply for a tourist e-Visa online prior to travel, or check for visa-on-arrival if they hold a valid visa/residency from US, UK, or Schengen countries.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of alcohol. Cash over USD 10,000 must be declared at customs on arrival.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping ( Baksheesh ) is deeply embedded. Expected for everything: restaurant servers, drivers, temple guides.
scam_alerts_common: Aggressive vendors at Giza Pyramids, camel ride price scams, fake papyrus shops, unmetered taxis.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress conservatively. Women should cover shoulders and knees in public. Head coverings required in mosques.
photography_restrictions: Strict ban on photographing military bases, police, Suez canal, or bridges. Drones strictly illegal.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 21+. Alcohol is sold only in licensed restaurants, bars, and select specialty shops.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens must apply for a Visitor Visa online prior to travel. Standard processing times take up to 20-30 working days.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 50 cigarettes, 3 bottles of wine or 1.125 liters of spirits. Extremely strict biosecurity: you must declare all food, hiking gear, and soil. Failing to declare carries instant AUD/NZD 400 fines.
tipping_culture_rules: No tipping/offensive. Tipping is not expected or standard in New Zealand.
scam_alerts_common: Campervan break-ins at isolated tourist viewpoints (never leave valuables in sight).
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Generally legal. Drone usage must comply with CAA Part 101 rules.
shoulder_season_months: March to May, September to November
major_national_closures: Anzac Day, Christmas Day, Waitangi Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Open containers are banned in many public spots (such as town centers overnight).

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. However, they can visit visa-free for tourism up to 180 days if holding a valid, used visa or permanent residency from US, UK, Schengen, Canada, or Japan.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 10 packs of cigarettes, 3 liters of alcohol. Cash over USD 10,000 must be declared at customs on arrival.
tipping_culture_rules: Expected 10-15% in restaurants and bars. Smaller tips for parking and supermarket baggers.
scam_alerts_common: Fake ATM fronts (skimming), corrupt transit police demanding cash bribes, overcharging taxis.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Strict rules on drone flights (foreigners cannot legally fly drones in Mexico without specific Mexican licenses).
major_national_closures: Day of the Dead (Nov 1-2, festive closures), Independence Day.
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Public consumption is illegal. Alcohol sales banned in dry law zones during elections.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a visa. Applications must be pre-arranged and submitted online and processed through a Brazilian consular office.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 400 cigarettes, 12 liters of alcohol. Cash over BRL 10,000 or equivalent must be declared at customs on arrival.
preferred_digital_wallets: Pix (requires local CPF, tourists use cards), PicPay, Apple Pay
tipping_culture_rules: Usually a 10% service charge is included in restaurant bills; extra tipping is optional.
tourist_transit_pass_name: Metrô Rio Card (Rio), Bilhete Único (Sao Paulo)
scam_alerts_common: Card skimming at public kiosks, pickpocketing on Copacabana beach, fake taxi apps.
emergency_indian_embassy_contact: Embassy of India, Brasilia: +55-61-3248-4006. Consulate General, Sao Paulo: +55-11-3279-0300.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: General rules. Strictly illegal to photograph military bases.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to November
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Public drinking is generally legal; open containers tolerated.

visa_requirements: Indian nationals require a visa. However, they can visit visa-free for up to 180 days for tourism if holding a valid visa or permanent residency from US, Canada, UK, Schengen, or Australia.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 20 packs of cigarettes, 3 liters of alcohol. Cash over USD 10,000 must be declared at customs on entry.
tipping_culture_rules: Optional; 10% is customary for good service in tourist restaurants.
scam_alerts_common: Taxis without meters overcharging, counterfeit currency notes (always check change), pickpocketing in crowded markets.
dress_code_restrictions: None. Dress warmly for high altitudes.
photography_restrictions: Photography inside museums and historical sites is restricted. Drones banned in Machu Picchu.
peak_tourism_months: May to September (Dry season in Andes)
monsoon_cyclone_months: Rainy Andes season: November to March
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Public consumption generally restricted in city streets.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require an Irish visa prior to travel. However, they can visit visa-free under the British-Irish Visa Scheme (BIVS) if holding a valid, used UK short-stay visa and arriving from the UK first.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR 10,000 or more is required upon entry.
tipping_culture_rules: Standard tipping is 10-12.5% in sit-down restaurants.
scam_alerts_common: Pickpocketing around Dublin Temple Bar, unlicensed taxi cabs late at night.
dress_code_restrictions: None.
photography_restrictions: Respect private property. Drones require IAA registration.
peak_tourism_months: June to August, March (St. Patrick's Day)
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Alcohol sales in stores restricted after 10 PM.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens require a Schengen Visa to visit Malta. Applications must be processed via VFS Global.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 1 liter of spirits. Declaring cash of EUR 10,000 or more is required upon entry.
tipping_culture_rules: Optional; leaving 10% cash is common in restaurants for good service.
scam_alerts_common: Overpriced airport taxi fares, pickpockets around Sliema ferry terminals.
dress_code_restrictions: Dress modestly inside historic cathedrals. Cover shoulders and knees.
photography_restrictions: Drones must be registered with CAD. Filming restrictions apply near security bases.
shoulder_season_months: April to May, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 17+. Widely sold in retail shops.

visa_requirements: Indian citizens are granted a free 4-month Visa-on-arrival in Fiji. Requirements: return ticket, passport valid for 6 months, and confirmed accommodation.
customs_duty_free_limits: Up to 200 cigarettes, 2.25 liters of spirits or 4.5 liters of beer. Currency limits: declare cash over FJD 10,000.
tipping_culture_rules: Tipping is not expected or custom; Fiji is a non-tipping society.
scam_alerts_common: Unlicensed tour boats, souvenir pricing inflation, Kava ceremony scams overcharging visitors.
dress_code_restrictions: Modest dress required in villages. Swimwear strictly for resort and beach zones.
photography_restrictions: Ask permission before taking photos of local villagers. Drone flights require CAAF clearance.
peak_tourism_months: July to August (Winter), December to January
shoulder_season_months: May to June, September to October
alcohol_availability_laws: Legal for 18+. Sold in resorts and licensed bars.
